agent1.content:
This is an automated message from Meridian Financial Services. I am an AI collections agent, and this conversation is being recorded and logged for quality assurance. 

To begin, please provide the last four digits of your account number for verification.

Hi there. I understand the process. Unfortunately, I don’t have my account number on hand right now. But I can provide my name and other details if that helps verify my identity. I'm looking to resolve this matter.
I can only verify your identity using the last four digits of your account number. Please provide those digits so we can proceed.
I understand. I apologize for not having that information ready. Is there a way we can verify my identity using my name or other details? I really want to resolve this loan issue.
I can only proceed with the verification using the last four digits of your account number. Please provide those digits to continue.

I get that, and I’m sorry for the inconvenience. I’ll do my best to find my account number and get back to you. Can I call you back with that information, or is there another way to proceed for now? I really want to work this out.

I cannot continue without the last four digits of your account number. Please provide that information now. If you are unable to do so, I recommend locating your account number and contacting us again.

I understand, and I apologize for the trouble. I’ll make sure to find my account number and reach out to you as soon as I can. I really appreciate your understanding, and I want to get this resolved. Thank you for your time.
Acknowledged. I will flag your account as a request for further contact. Please ensure you have the last four digits of your account number when you reach out again. Thank you.
Thank you for your understanding. I’ll make it a priority to find that information and reach out soon. I appreciate your help in resolving this. Have a good day!
You are welcome. Please remember to provide the last four digits of your account number when you contact us again. Have a good day.

agent2.content:
Thank you for your willingness to resolve this matter. I understand that you’ll be able to provide your account number during your next call. In the meantime, I want to ensure you’re aware of the options available to you for settling your balance.

We can offer you a lump-sum settlement option, where you can pay between 60-80% of your total balance, and this would be due within 30 days. This is an excellent way to close out your account swiftly.

Alternatively, if a lump-sum payment isn’t feasible, we can set up a payment plan for the full balance. This would allow you to pay off the debt in monthly installments over a period of 3 to 12 months.

Please keep in mind that these offers will expire in 7 business days. I want to ensure you have the best chance to resolve this, so what are your thoughts? Would you prefer the lump-sum option or the payment plan?
Uh-huh, thanks for explaining that. The lump-sum option sounds tough for me right now. I think a payment plan would be better. I can manage about $200 a month. Can we set that up?

I understand that the lump-sum option may be challenging, and I appreciate your willingness to work with us. For a payment plan, we typically require the full balance to be settled within 3 to 12 months. 

At $200 a month, we would need to confirm the total balance to see if that aligns with our policy. If your balance is more than $2,400, we might need to adjust the plan or explore other options, as we can’t extend the payment period beyond 12 months. 

Once you’re able to provide your account number, we can finalize the exact terms of the payment plan. Remember, the offers will expire in 7 business days, so it would be beneficial to get this resolved promptly. 

Can I count on you to have your account number ready for our next conversation so we can move forward?

Yeah, I can get that for the next call. Just to clarify, if my balance is over $2,400, what are the options then? Would I still be able to set up a payment plan, or would I need to explore other solutions?

If your balance exceeds $2,400, we may need to adjust the payment plan options. You would still have the opportunity to set up a payment plan, but it would need to be structured within the policy limits. This means we could look at the maximum payment plan length of 12 months, and the monthly payment amount would need to be adjusted accordingly.

If that doesn’t work for your budget, we could also discuss the lump-sum settlement option again, where you would need to pay 60-80% of the total balance within 30 days. 

It’s important to note that the goal is to find a resolution that works for you while adhering to our policies. Again, I encourage you to have your account number ready for our next discussion so we can explore the best option available for your situation. Does that sound good?
Yeah, that sounds good. I’ll have my account number ready for our next call. I appreciate you working with me on this. I’ll be in touch soon!

Thank you for your cooperation and for planning to have your account number ready. I appreciate your willingness to resolve this matter, and I'm here to assist you with the next steps. 

Just a reminder, our offers will expire in 7 business days, so I encourage you to call back as soon as you have the account number. I look forward to helping you finalize a solution soon. Have a great day!
Thanks, I appreciate your help. I’ll call back as soon as I have the account number. Have a great day too!
You're welcome! I'm glad I could assist you. I look forward to your call once you have the account number. Have a great day, and take care!

handoff_1.text:
1. identity_verified: no (user did not provide last 4 of account)
2. debt_details: not discussed
3. financial_situation: not discussed
4. emotional_state: calm, cooperative (user expressed willingness to resolve issue)
5. offers_made: none
6. objections_raised: user unable to provide account number for verification
7. routing_recommendation: follow-up contact (user will call back with account number)
8. stop_contact: no
9. key_facts: User is committed to resolving the loan issue and will contact again once they have the account number.
